weeks.weekly_summary: Establish aerobic base with 2 sessions per week. Focus on easy running and one quality tempo session to build lactate threshold. Recent Strava data shows ~15.7 km over 3 activities; this week will consolidate to structured 2-session format.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent running has been easy to moderate intensity. Football session (158 avg HR, 188 max HR) shows good cardiovascular capacity. This week introduces structured tempo work. Sessions scheduled Monday (easy) and Wednesday (tempo) to space stimulus and respect 4-hour weekly budget. Rest on other running days; cross-training (football, pilates, gym) locked on Sat/Tue/Fri.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 will increase total volume to ~26 km (8-10% growth), adding a second quality session or extending long run. Tempo intensity repeats into week 2.

days.coach_analysis: Good commitment to the plan—49 minutes of gym work with an average HR of 111 bpm suggests you kept the intensity controlled, which is exactly right for a cross-training day. This light-to-moderate effort will build supportive strength without hammering your central nervous system before your next running session. You're balancing the training week well.

days.notes: Fixed cross-training commitment
days.coach_analysis: Solid execution of your football commitment—you maintained an aerobic-focused effort at 158 bpm average, which is right in that sustainable cross-training zone for building work capacity without compromising your running. The 5.75 km over 68 minutes shows you stayed engaged with good intensity variation typical of football, hitting the aerobic and leg-strength targets you planned for. This session delivered exactly what you needed.

days.notes: Fixed cross-training commitment. Gentle activity—can pair with easy running day.

days.title: Tempo 14km (2km easy + 10km @ 2:42/km + 2km easy)
days.description: Continuous tempo run at lactate threshold pace (2:42/km). Warm up 2km easy (3:40/km), sustain 10km @ 2:42/km, cool down 2km easy (3:40/km). This is your quality session for the week.
days.notes: First quality session. Keep effort steady; should be 'comfortably hard.' Focus on pacing discipline.

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume increases ~8% to 26 km. Maintain Monday easy run, shift tempo to Wednesday. Introduce slightly longer sustained effort while respecting 4-hour weekly budget. Quality session remains at tempo threshold intensity.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 1 completed solidly. Monday easy + Wednesday tempo pattern proven sustainable. Week 2 adds ~2 km total (8% growth): extending Monday easy to 11km and Wednesday tempo to 15km. Cross-training (football, pilates, gym) remains locked on Sat/Tue/Fri. No consecutive hard sessions or hard work day before/after any extended run.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 continues building: ~5-8% volume growth. Consider introducing fartlek or short intervals into the tempo work, or extend long run if polarization allows.

days.title: Easy 11km
days.description: Easy aerobic run at 3:40/km. Conversational pace. Slightly longer than last week to build aerobic capacity.
days.notes: Post-football recovery. Maintain steady Zone 1-2 effort.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Core stability, flexibility, injury prevention.
days.notes: Fixed cross-training commitment

days.title: Tempo 15km (2km easy + 11km @ 2:42/km + 2km easy)
days.description: Extended tempo run at lactate threshold pace (2:42/km). Warm up 2km easy (3:40/km), sustain 11km @ 2:42/km, cool down 2km easy (3:40/km). Build comfort at threshold intensity.
days.notes: Quality session. Increase sustained effort from Week 1 (10km → 11km at tempo). Focus on even pacing and breathing control.

weeks.weekly_summary: Peak week of the block. Volume increases ~5-8% to ~28 km. Maintain dual quality sessions (Monday easy + Wednesday tempo), with slight extensions to both. Build aerobic capacity before recovery week.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 reaches peak volume for the 4-week block at ~28 km (5-8% growth over Week 2's 26 km). Monday easy extends to 12km; Wednesday tempo extends to 16km (2km + 12km @ tempo + 2km). This is the highest-intensity week before Week 4 recovery. Cross-training pattern unchanged (Sat/Tue/Fri). Monitor fatigue; if perceived exertion feels unsustainable, reduce Wednesday tempo to 15km and reassess.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is RECOVERY WEEK: reduce volume by 20-25% to ~21-22 km. Keep one quality session (Wednesday tempo at 10km). Long run (Monday) cut to 9-10km. Taper begins in Week 4.

days.title: Easy 12km
days.description: Easy aerobic run at 3:40/km. Conversational pace. Peak easy-run distance for the block.
days.notes: Post-football recovery. Maintain steady Zone 1-2 effort. This is the longest easy run of the block.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Core stability, flexibility, injury prevention.
days.notes: Fixed cross-training commitment

days.title: Tempo 16km (2km easy + 12km @ 2:42/km + 2km easy)
days.description: Extended tempo run at lactate threshold pace (2:42/km). Warm up 2km easy (3:40/km), sustain 12km @ 2:42/km, cool down 2km easy (3:40/km). Peak tempo effort for the block.
days.notes: Quality session. Peak tempo effort (12km @ 2:42/km). This is the hardest session in the block. Focus on steady pacing, breathing, and mental resilience.

weeks.weekly_summary: RECOVERY WEEK. Volume reduced by 20-25% to ~21 km. Maintain one quality session (Wednesday tempo shortened to 10km), easy run reduced to 9-10km. Allow adaptive response to training stimulus and prepare for next block.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 is a recovery/consolidation week. Volume drops to 21 km (~25% reduction from Week 3's 28 km), following the 4-week periodization model. Monday easy reduced from 12km to 9km. Wednesday tempo shortened from 16km to 10km total (2km + 6km @ 2:42/km + 2km), maintaining threshold stimulus but reducing absolute load. This week allows CNS recovery, tissue adaptation, and supercompensation before the next block (Weeks 5-8: Aerobic Development phase, likely beginning long run development and progressive intensity). Monitor fatigue; if athlete reports low energy, reduce Wednesday tempo to 8km (2km + 4km @ 2:42/km + 2km).
weeks.next_week_preview: Next 4-week block (Weeks 5-8) enters Aerobic Development phase. Expect introduction of long runs (beginning ~8-10km, growing toward 14-16km by Week 8), progressive tempo runs or fartlek, and slight increase in weekly volume (toward 5 hours/week as race nears). Quality sessions shift toward longer sustained efforts and early marathon-pace work.

days.title: Tempo 10km (2km easy + 6km @ 2:42/km + 2km easy)
days.description: Reduced tempo run at lactate threshold pace (2:42/km). Warm up 2km easy (3:40/km), sustain 6km @ 2:42/km, cool down 2km easy (3:40/km). Maintain threshold stimulus while reducing total load.
days.notes: Recovery-week quality session. Shorten sustained effort (6km vs 12km last week) to allow adaptation while keeping threshold neural patterns active.

coaching_overview: This 4-week Base Building block (Weeks 1-4 of the 14-week half-marathon plan) establishes aerobic foundation through polarized training: 80% easy, 20% hard. The athlete starts with 2 runs/week (Monday easy + Wednesday tempo) on a 4-hour weekly time budget, respecting preferred running days and locked cross-training commitments (football Sat, pilates Tue, gym Fri). Volume progression follows the 10% rule: Week 1 (24 km) → Week 2 (+8% → 26 km) → Week 3 (+8% → 28 km, peak) → Week 4 (−25% recovery → 21 km). Quality sessions are tempo runs at 2:42/km (lactate threshold), scaled from 10km sustained (Week 1) to 12km (Week 3 peak) to 6km (Week 4 recovery). Recent Strava data (15.7 km over 3 activities) shows the athlete is well-rested and capable of this load; football session (avg HR 158, max HR 188) indicates good aerobic fitness. The block builds structural aerobic capacity, steady-state lactate threshold tolerance, and training consistency before transitioning to Aerobic Development (Weeks 5-8) with longer runs and progressive marathon-pace work. Total plan distance: 99 km over 4 weeks.
weekly_summary: Establish aerobic base with 2 sessions per week. Focus on easy running and one quality tempo session to build lactate threshold. Recent Strava data shows ~15.7 km over 3 activities; this week will consolidate to structured 2-session format.
coaching_notes: Recent running has been easy to moderate intensity. Football session (158 avg HR, 188 max HR) shows good cardiovascular capacity. This week introduces structured tempo work. Sessions scheduled Monday (easy) and Wednesday (tempo) to space stimulus and respect 4-hour weekly budget. Rest on other running days; cross-training (football, pilates, gym) locked on Sat/Tue/Fri.
